executive_summary: Trump signals Iran war 'close to over' while global markets hit record highs, though policymakers warn investors underestimate war's economic toll. China's EV maker BYD faces scrutiny over a Shenzhen garage fire, while hybrid threats intensify with pro-Russian groups targeting Swedish critical infrastructure.

key_insights.insight: Trump claims Iran war 'close to over' as S&P 500 reaches record high and oil markets remain volatile
Policymakers warn markets are underestimating the economic costs of ongoing conflicts globally
BYD reports fire incident at Shenzhen garage for test and scrapped vehicles, raising safety questions
Pro-Russian group targets Swedish power plant in 2025 cyberattack, highlighting critical infrastructure vulnerabilities
US investigating suspicious oil trades made before Trump's policy pivots, suggesting potential market manipulation
Public sentiment souring on AI as major firms Anthropic and OpenAI prepare IPOs amid heavy tech spending
El Salvador enacts law allowing life sentences for minors as young as 12, drawing human rights criticism
Silver market faces sixth consecutive annual deficit, indicating ongoing supply constraints
Ye postpones Marseille concert after French authorities announce plans to seek performance ban
Record momentum trading by BofA stock traders reflects heightened market activity amid geopolitical uncertainty

blind_spot: Rising cyberattacks on critical energy infrastructure across Europe by state-linked groups go largely unreported despite posing significant risks to energy security and NATO cohesion
